How do I reverse a string in JavaScript?

1542

I have the string "Hello World" and I need to reverse it to get "dlroW olleH".

What is the most efficient way to reverse a string in JavaScript? I want to avoid using any external libraries if possible. 

const str = "Hello World";
// How to reverse this?
console.log(str);
javascript string reverse
2103

The most concise and idiomatic way is to split the string into an array, reverse it, and join it back: 

function reverseString(str) {
  return str.split('').reverse().join('');
}

reverseString('Hello World'); // "dlroW olleH"

This works by converting the string to an array of characters, reversing the array in place, then joining the characters back into a string.

487

You can also use the spread operator and Array.from for better Unicode handling: 

const reverseString = str => [...str].reverse().join('');

// Or with Array.from:
const reverseString2 = str => Array.from(str).reverse().join('');

This approach correctly handles multi-byte characters and emoji.

112

A recursive approach, if you prefer functional style: 

function reverseString(str) {
  if (str.length <= 1) return str;
  return reverseString(str.slice(1)) + str[0];
}

Note: this is less efficient than the array approach for long strings.
